project: Atlas - Assassin's Creed Shadows Interactive Map

deploymentState.summary: The canonical 0.9.4.2 release and local browser matrix are coherent. Public GitHub Pages verification and physical iPad/desktop verification remain required for the optimization package.

gates: public deployment and physical-device publish gate
controls: Pull requests validate their own ref
Runtime component versions must match source, HTML, cache and validation
Behavioural boundary assertions are preferred over brittle duplicate literals
High-risk changes remain isolated until required checks pass

evidence: CONFLICT-REPORT.md reports pass with zero critical and zero high findings
data/conflict-reports/browser-matrix.json reports 372 passing checks across six profiles

currentHighPriority: 0.9.4.3: merge monitor contract and scan-health workflow repairs after high-risk validation
0.9.4.3: verify public GitHub Pages and physical iPad/desktop behaviour
0.9.4.4: consolidate CSS cascade ownership and lower the conflict risk score
0.9.4.4: make generated reports artifact-first and commit semantic changes only
0.9.4.5: perform the scheduled full audit and resume ultra-HD map calibration work
Convert authorised derived evidence into map anchors after the map coordinate gate is stable
knownDeferred: Next creator intake after current authorised evidence integration and explicit authorisation
Full evidence-based location anchoring after the ultra-HD map stage gate
Actual original Atlas display font assets beyond CSS aliases and fallbacks
Full completeness audit against 完整版 and 终极完整版 requirements

knownTechnicalDebt.evidence: CONFLICT-REPORT.md reports 25 warnings and a 50/100 risk score, mainly duplicate transition, transform and backdrop-filter ownership.
Repository and Chromium profile evidence exists, but current public deployment and physical-device evidence is not yet recorded.

knownRegressions.acceptedBehaviourAffected: The public release should load and display the intended cumulative release implementation.
evidence: release-manifest.json is the canonical release source
atlas-bootstrap.js owns visible version and service-worker registration
browser matrix confirms one 0.9.4.2 cache version across release scripts

knownRegressions.acceptedBehaviourAffected: Realtime monitor source, displayed timing, cache version and validation must describe the same bounded heartbeat and polling behaviour.
evidence: Main scan health report is fail with 35 of 42 checks passing
Main bridge source, HTML cache query, footer copy and validation workflow use conflicting component versions and intervals
Optimization branch aligns bridge 0.3.1, HTML, canonical release cache namespace and validation expectations
closureRequirements: Physical iPad and desktop verification is recorded where applicable

knownRegressions.acceptedBehaviourAffected: A pull request must validate the proposed code rather than a forced checkout of main.
evidence: Previous validate-scan-system workflow explicitly checked out ref: main
Optimization branch removes the forced ref and adds pull_request coverage plus validation_current_ref

knownRegressions.acceptedBehaviourAffected: Health automation should not generate repeated repository commits when only generatedAt changes.
evidence: Recent main history contains repeated Update scan system health report [skip ci] commits
Optimization branch compares report semantics without generatedAt before persisting

auditHistory.result: Roadmap and recurring audit policy established
Deployment version-source mismatch confirmed; release reclassified as regressed and unverified.

Core 0.9.4.2 browser matrix passed, authorised Dada catalogue completed, and three workflow/runtime regressions were identified and repaired on an isolated optimization branch. Public deployment and physical-device closure evidence remain pending.

nextThreeReleasePriorities.0.9.4.3: Validate and deploy the fast-safe monitor and workflow baseline; close current regressions with public and device evidence.
nextThreeReleasePriorities.0.9.4.4: Reduce CSS/runtime ownership warnings and generated-report maintenance cost.
nextThreeReleasePriorities.0.9.4.5: Run the next scheduled full audit and resume ultra-HD map base and coordinate calibration work.
